text: Glory be to the One Who took His servant ňĻMuŠł•ammadňļ by night from the Sacred Mosque to the Farthest Mosque whose surroundings We have blessed, so that We may show him some of Our signs. <sup foot_note=516>516</sup> Indeed, He <sup foot_note=517>517</sup> alone is the All-Hearing, All-Seeing.

foot_notes.516: Al-Isr√Ę ' refers to the Prophet‚Äôs Night Journey from Mecca to Jerusalem about a year before his emigration (or Hijrah ) from Mecca to Medina. This journey came as a comfort for the Prophet after several years of hardship and persecution, which included a 3-year siege by Meccan pagans, who drove the Muslims out of the city and forbade anyone from trading, marrying, or feeding them. This was followed by the ‚ÄúYear of Sadness,‚ÄĚ which included the death of the Prophet‚Äôs uncle Abu ŠĻ¨√Ęlib, the major defender of MuŠł•ammad ( Ô∑ļ ) despite his disbelief in his message, as well as the death of the Prophet‚Äôs beloved wife Khad√ģjah. The Prophet was carried overnight by a noble steed (called Bur√Ęq ) from Mecca to Jerusalem where he met some earlier prophets and led them in prayer. He was later carried to the heavens (this journey is called Al-Mi‚Äôr√Ęj , or the Ascension) where he received direct orders from Allah to observe five daily prayers. The Ascension is referred to in 53:13‚Äď18.

text: And We gave Moses the Scripture and made it a guide for the Children of Israel, ňĻstating:ňļ ‚ÄúDo not take besides Me any other Trustee of Affairs,
ňĻOňļ descendants of those We carried with Noah ňĻin the Arkňļ! He was indeed a grateful servant.‚ÄĚ
And We warned the Children of Israel in the Scripture, ‚ÄúYou will certainly cause corruption in the land twice, and you will become extremely arrogant.
‚ÄúWhen the first of the two warnings would come to pass, We would send against you some of Our servants of great might, who would ravage your homes. This would be a warning fulfilled.
Then ňĻafter your repentanceňļ We would give you the upper hand over them and aid you with wealth and offspring, causing you to outnumber them.

If you act rightly, it is for your own good, but if you do wrong, it is to your own loss. And when the second warning would come to pass, your enemies would ňĻbe left toňļ totally disgrace you and enter the Temple ňĻof Jerusalemňļ as they entered it the first time, and utterly destroy whatever would fall into their hands.
Perhaps your Lord will have mercy on you ňĻif you repentňļ, but if you return ňĻto sinňļ, We will return ňĻto punishmentňļ. And We have made Hell a ňĻpermanentňļ confinement for the disbelievers.‚ÄĚ
Surely this Quran guides to what is most upright, and gives good news to the believers‚ÄĒwho do good‚ÄĒthat they will have a mighty reward.
And ňĻit warnsňļ those who do not believe in the Hereafter ňĻthatňļ We have prepared for them a painful punishment.
And humans ňĻswiftlyňļ pray for evil <sup foot_note=518>518</sup> as they pray for good. For humankind is ever hasty.

foot_notes.518: Some people are quick to pray against themselves or others in times of anger and frustration.

text: We made the day and night as two signs. So We made the sign of the night devoid of light, and We made the sign of the day ňĻperfectlyňļ bright, so that you may seek the bounty of your Lord and know the number of years and calculation ňĻof timeňļ. And We have explained everything in detail.
We have bound every human‚Äôs destiny to their neck. <sup foot_note=519>519</sup> And on the Day of Judgment We will bring forth to each ňĻpersonňļ a record which they will find laid open.

foot_notes.519: This refers to the deeds that one is destined to do of their own free will. Out of His infinite knowledge, Allah knows people‚Äôs choices even before they make them. In the Hereafter, He will raise them from the dead for judgment.

text: ňĻAnd it will be said,ňļ ‚ÄúRead your record. You ňĻaloneňļ are sufficient this Day to take account of yourself.‚ÄĚ

Whoever chooses to be guided, it is only for their own good. And whoever chooses to stray, it is only to their own loss. No soul burdened with sin will bear the burden of another. And We would never punish ňĻa peopleňļ until We have sent a messenger ňĻto warn themňļ.
Whenever We intend to destroy a society, We command its elite ňĻto obey Allahňļ but they act rebelliously in it. So the decree ňĻof punishmentňļ is justified, and We destroy it utterly.
ňĻImagineňļ how many peoples We have destroyed after Noah! And sufficient is your Lord as All-Aware and All-Seeing of the sins of His servants.
Whoever desires this fleeting world ňĻaloneňļ, We hasten in it whatever We please to whoever We will; then We destine them for Hell, where they will burn, condemned and rejected.
But whoever desires the Hereafter and strives for it accordingly, and is a ňĻtrueňļ believer, it is they whose striving will be appreciated.
We provide both the former and the latter from the bounty of your Lord. And the bounty of your Lord can never be withheld.
See how We have favoured some over others ňĻin this lifeňļ, but the Hereafter is certainly far greater in rank and in favour.
Do not set up any other god with Allah, or you will end up condemned, abandoned.

For your Lord has decreed that you worship none but Him. And honour your parents. If one or both of them reach old age in your care, never say to them ňĻevenňļ ‚Äėugh,‚Äô nor yell at them. Rather, address them respectfully.
And be humble with them out of mercy, and pray, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Be merciful to them as they raised me when I was young.‚ÄĚ
Your Lord knows best what is within yourselves. If you are righteous, He is certainly All-Forgiving to those who ňĻconstantlyňļ turn to Him.
Give to close relatives their due, as well as the poor and ňĻneedyňļ travellers. And do not spend wastefully.
Surely the wasteful are ňĻlikeňļ brothers to the devils. And the Devil is ever ungrateful to his Lord.
But if you must turn them down ňĻbecause you lack the means to giveňļ‚ÄĒwhile hoping to receive your Lord‚Äôs bounty‚ÄĒthen ňĻat leastňļ give them a kind word.
Do not be so tight-fisted, for you will be blameworthy; nor so open-handed, for you will end up in poverty.
Surely your Lord gives abundant or limited provisions to whoever He wills. He is certainly All-Aware, All-Seeing of His servants.
Do not kill your children for fear of poverty. We provide for them and for you. Surely killing them is a heinous sin.
Do not go near adultery. It is truly a shameful deed and an evil way.

Do not take a ňĻhumanňļ life‚ÄĒmade sacred by Allah‚ÄĒexcept with ňĻlegalňļ right. <sup foot_note=520>520</sup> If anyone is killed unjustly, We have given their heirs <sup foot_note=521>521</sup> the authority, but do not let them exceed limits in retaliation, <sup foot_note=522>522</sup> for they are already supported ňĻby lawňļ.

foot_notes.520: For example, in retaliation for intentional killing through legal channels.
foot_notes.521: Here the word wali, ‚Äúheir,‚ÄĚ actually means awliy√Ę ' or ‚Äúheirs,‚ÄĚ which includes the closest relatives, both men and women.
foot_notes.522: By killing anyone other than the killer, or killing others with the killer.

text: Do not come near the wealth of the orphan‚ÄĒunless intending to enhance it‚ÄĒuntil they attain maturity. And honour ňĻyourňļ pledges, for you will surely be accountable for them.
Give in full when you measure, and weigh with an even balance. That is fairest and best in the end.
Do not follow what you have no ňĻsureňļ knowledge of. Indeed, all will be called to account for ňĻtheirňļ hearing, sight, and intellect.
And do not walk on the earth arrogantly. Surely you can neither crack the earth nor stretch to the height of the mountains.
The violation of any of these ňĻcommandmentsňļ is detestable to your Lord.
This is part of the wisdom which your Lord has revealed to you ňĻO Prophetňļ. And do not set up any other god with Allah ňĻO humanityňļ, or you will be cast into Hell, blameworthy, rejected.
Has your Lord favoured you ňĻpagansňļ with sons and taken angels as ňĻHisňļ daughters? <sup foot_note=523>523</sup> You are truly making an outrageous claim.

foot_notes.523: Some pagans had the belief that the angels are the daughters of Allah.

text: We have surely varied ňĻthe signsňļ in this Quran so perhaps they may be mindful, but it only drives them farther away.
Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúHad there been other gods besides Him‚ÄĒas they claim‚ÄĒthen they would have certainly sought a way to ňĻchallengeňļ the Lord of the Throne.‚ÄĚ
Glorified and Highly Exalted is He above what they claim!

The seven heavens, the earth, and all those in them glorify Him. There is not a single thing that does not glorify His praises‚ÄĒbut you ňĻsimplyňļ cannot comprehend their glorification. He is indeed Most Forbearing, All-Forgiving.
When you ňĻO Prophetňļ recite the Quran, We put a hidden barrier between you and those who do not believe in the Hereafter.
We have cast veils over their hearts‚ÄĒleaving them unable to comprehend it‚ÄĒand deafness in their ears. And when you mention your Lord alone in the Quran, they turn their backs in aversion.
We know best how they listen to your recitation and what they say privately‚ÄĒwhen the wrongdoers say, ‚ÄúYou would only be following a bewitched man.‚ÄĚ
See how they call you names ňĻO Prophetňļ! <sup foot_note=524>524</sup> So they have gone so ňĻfarňļ astray that they cannot find the ňĻRightňļ Way.

foot_notes.524: They rejected you as a magician, a poet, a fabricator, and madman.

text: And they say ňĻmockinglyňļ, ‚ÄúWhen we are reduced to bones and ashes, will we really be raised as a new creation?‚ÄĚ
Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúňĻYes, even ifňļ you become stones, or iron,

or whatever you think is harder to bring to life!‚ÄĚ Then they will ask ňĻyouňļ, ‚ÄúWho will bring us back ňĻto lifeňļ?‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúThe One Who created you the first time.‚ÄĚ They will then shake their heads at you and ask, ‚ÄúWhen will that be?‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúPerhaps it is soon!‚ÄĚ
On the Day He will call you, you will ňĻinstantlyňļ respond by praising Him, <sup foot_note=525>525</sup> thinking you had remained ňĻin the worldňļ only for a little while.

foot_notes.525: When everyone is raised from the dead, they will all praise Allah, regardless of what they had believed in this life.

text: Tell My ňĻbelievingňļ servants to say only what is best. Satan certainly seeks to sow discord among them. Satan is indeed a sworn enemy to humankind.
Your Lord knows you best. He may have mercy on you if He wills, or punish you if He wills. We have not sent you ňĻO Prophetňļ as a keeper over them.
Your Lord knows best all those in the heavens and the earth. And We have surely favoured some prophets above others, and to David We gave the Psalms. <sup foot_note=526>526</sup>

foot_notes.526: The reason David is singled out here is because some Jewish authorities at the time of the Prophet ( Ô∑ļ ) claimed that no scripture had been revealed after Moses. So this verse refutes this claim by referring to the Psalms of David.

text: Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúInvoke those you claim ňĻto be divineňļ <sup foot_note=527>527</sup> besides Him‚ÄĒthey do not have the power to undo harm from you or transfer it ňĻto someone elseňļ.‚ÄĚ

foot_notes.527: i.e., Jesus, Ezra, and the angels. Idol worship is refuted in other passages such as 7:191-198 and 34:22.

text: ňĻEvenňļ the closest ňĻto Allahňļ of those invoked would be seeking a way to their Lord, hoping for His mercy, and fearing His punishment. Indeed, your Lord‚Äôs torment is fearsome.
There is not a ňĻwickedňļ society that We will not destroy or punish with a severe torment before the Day of Judgment. That is written in the Record.

Nothing keeps Us from sending the ňĻdemandedňļ signs except that they had ňĻalreadyňļ been denied by earlier peoples. And We gave Tham√Ľd the she-camel as a clear sign, but they wrongfully rejected it. <sup foot_note=528>528</sup> We only send the signs as a warning.

foot_notes.528: Or they did wrong by it.

text: And ňĻremember, O Prophetňļ when We told you, ‚ÄúCertainly your Lord encompasses the people.‚ÄĚ And We have made what We brought you to see <sup foot_note=529>529</sup> as well as the cursed tree ňĻmentionedňļ in the Quran <sup foot_note=530>530</sup> only as a test for the people. We keep warning them, but it only increases them greatly in defiance.

foot_notes.529: During the Night Journey mentioned in 17:1.
foot_notes.530: The tree of Zaqq√Ľm which grows in the depths of Hell as mentioned in 37:62-65. The pagans of Mecca used to make fun of the Prophet ( Ô∑ļ ) and say, ‚ÄúHow can a tree grow in Hell?‚ÄĚ

text: And ňĻrememberňļ when We said to the angels, ‚ÄúProstrate before Adam,‚ÄĚ so they all did‚ÄĒbut not Ibl√ģs , <sup foot_note=531>531</sup> who protested, ‚ÄúShould I prostrate to the one You have created from mud?‚ÄĚ
Adding, ‚ÄúDo you see this one you honoured above me? If you delay my end until the Day of Judgment, I will certainly take hold of his descendants, except for a few.‚ÄĚ
Allah responded, ‚ÄúBe gone! Whoever of them follows you, Hell will surely be the reward for all of you‚ÄĒan ample reward.

And incite whoever you can of them with your voice, mobilize against them all your cavalry and infantry, manipulate them in their wealth and children, and make them promises.‚ÄĚ But Satan promises them nothing but delusion.
ňĻAllah added,ňļ ‚ÄúYou will truly have no authority over My ňĻfaithfulňļ servants.‚ÄĚ And sufficient is your Lord as a Guardian.
It is your Lord Who steers the ships for you through the sea, so that you may seek His bounty. Surely He is ever Merciful to you.

When you are touched with hardship at sea, you ňĻtotallyňļ forget all ňĻthe godsňļ you ňĻnormallyňļ invoke, except Him. But when He delivers you ňĻsafelyňļ to shore, you turn away. Humankind is ever ungrateful.
Do you feel secure that He will not cause the land to swallow you up, or unleash upon you a storm of stones? Then you will find none to protect you.
Or do you feel secure that He will not send you back to sea once again, and send upon you a violent storm, drowning you for your denial? Then you will find none to avenge you against Us.
Indeed, We have dignified the children of Adam, carried them on land and sea, granted them good and lawful provisions, and privileged them far above many of Our creatures.

ňĻBeware ofňļ the Day We will summon every people with their leader. <sup foot_note=532>532</sup> So whoever will be given their record in their right hand will read it ňĻhappilyňļ and will not be wronged ňĻeven by the width ofňļ the thread of a date stone.

foot_notes.532: Other meanings include: with their record of deeds, or their prophet, or their scripture.

text: But whoever is blind ňĻto the truthňļ in this ňĻworldňļ will be blind in the Hereafter, and ňĻevenňļ far more astray from the ňĻRightňļ Way.

They definitely ňĻthought theyňļ were about to lure you away from what We have revealed to you ňĻO Prophetňļ, hoping that you would attribute something else to Us falsely‚ÄĒand then they would have certainly taken you as a close friend. <sup foot_note=533>533</sup>

foot_notes.533: The pagans of Mecca tried in vain to dissuade the Prophet ( Ô∑ļ ) from preaching the message of Islam. In some instances they offered to worship his Allah if he agreed to bow to their idols, and some even offered him riches and positions if he just stopped preaching in the city.

text: Had We not made you steadfast, you probably would have inclined to them a little,
and then We truly would have made you taste double ňĻpunishmentňļ both in this life and after death, and you would have found no helper against Us.
They were about to intimidate you to drive you out of the land ňĻof Meccaňļ, but then they would not have survived after you ňĻhad leftňļ except for a little while.
ňĻThis has beenňļ Our way with the messengers We sent before you. And you will never find any change in Our way.
Observe the prayer from the decline of the sun until the darkness of the night and the dawn prayer, for certainly the dawn prayer is witnessed ňĻby angelsňļ. <sup foot_note=534>534</sup>

foot_notes.534: This verse gives the times of the five daily prayers: the decline of the sun refers to the afternoon and late afternoon prayers, the darkness of night refers to sunset and late evening prayers, then the dawn prayer.

text: And rise at ňĻthe lastňļ part of the night, offering additional prayers, so your Lord may raise you to a station of praise. <sup foot_note=535>535</sup>

foot_notes.535: This refers to the time when the Prophet ( Ô∑ļ ) will make intercession ( shaf√Ę‚Äôah ) on the Day of Judgment.

text: And say, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Grant me an honourable entrance and an honourable exit <sup foot_note=536>536</sup> and give me a supporting authority from Yourself.‚ÄĚ

foot_notes.536: This verse was revealed when the Prophet ( Ô∑ļ ) received the order to emigrate from Mecca to Medina. So here he ( Ô∑ļ ) is praying to leave Mecca honourably and enter Medina honourably. It can also apply to any worldly activity that a person starts and finishes.

text: And declare, ‚ÄúThe truth has come and falsehood has vanished. Indeed, falsehood is bound to vanish.‚ÄĚ
We send down the Quran as a healing and mercy for the believers, but it only increases the wrongdoers in loss.
When We grant people Our favours, they turn away, acting arrogantly. But when touched with evil, they lose all hope.
Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúEveryone acts in their own way. But your Lord knows best whose way is rightly guided.‚ÄĚ
They ask you ňĻO Prophetňļ about the spirit. Say, ‚ÄúIts nature is known only to my Lord, and you ňĻO humanityňļ have been given but little knowledge.‚ÄĚ
If We willed, We could have certainly taken away what We have revealed to you ňĻO Prophetňļ‚ÄĒthen you would find none to guarantee its return from Us‚ÄĒ
had it not been for the mercy of your Lord. Indeed, His favour upon you is immense.
Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúIf ňĻallňļ humans and jinn were to come together to produce the equivalent of this Quran, they could not produce its equal, no matter how they supported each other.‚ÄĚ
And We have truly set forth every ňĻkind ofňļ lesson for humanity in this Quran, yet most people persist in disbelief.
They challenge ňĻthe Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúWe will never believe in you until you cause a spring to gush forth from the earth for us,
or until you have a garden of palm trees and vineyards, and cause rivers to flow abundantly in it,
or cause the sky to fall upon us in pieces, as you have claimed, or bring Allah and the angels before us, face to face,

or until you have a house of gold, or you ascend into heaven‚ÄĒand even then we will not believe in your ascension until you bring down to us a book that we can read.‚ÄĚ Say, ‚ÄúGlory be to my Lord! Am I not only a human messenger?‚ÄĚ
And nothing has prevented people from believing when guidance comes to them except their protest: ‚ÄúHas Allah sent a human as a messenger?‚ÄĚ
Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúHad there been angels walking the earth, well settled, We would have surely sent down for them an angel from heaven as a messenger.‚ÄĚ
Say, ‚ÄúSufficient is Allah as a Witness between me and you. He is certainly All-Knowing, All-Seeing of His servants.‚ÄĚ

Whoever Allah guides is truly guided. And whoever He leaves to stray, you will find no guardians for them besides Him. And We will drag them on their faces on the Day of Judgment‚ÄĒdeaf, dumb, and blind. Hell will be their home. Whenever it dies down, We will flare it up for them.
That is their reward for rejecting Our signs and asking ňĻmockinglyňļ, ‚ÄúWhen we are reduced to bones and ashes, will we really be raised as a new creation?‚ÄĚ

Have they not realized that Allah, Who created the heavens and the earth, can ňĻeasilyňļ re-create them? He has ňĻalreadyňļ set for them a time, about which there is no doubt. But the wrongdoers persist in denial.

Say ňĻto them, O Prophetňļ, ‚ÄúEven if you were to possess the ňĻinfiniteňļ treasuries of my Lord‚Äôs mercy, then you would certainly withhold ňĻthemňļ, fearing they would run out‚ÄĒfor humankind is ever stingy!‚ÄĚ

We surely gave Moses nine clear signs. <sup foot_note=537>537</sup> ňĻYou, O Prophet, canňļ ask the Children of Israel. When Moses came to them, Pharaoh said to him, ‚ÄúI really think that you, O Moses, are bewitched.‚ÄĚ

foot_notes.537: The nine signs of Moses are: the staff, the hand (both mentioned in 20:17-22), famine, shortage of crops, floods, locusts, lice, frogs, and blood (all mentioned in 7:130-133). These signs came as proofs for Pharaoh and the Egyptians. Otherwise, Moses had some other signs such water gushing out of the rock after he hit it with his staff, and splitting the sea.

text: Moses replied, ‚ÄúYou know well that none has sent these ňĻsignsňļ down except the Lord of the heavens and the earth as insights. And I really think that you, O Pharaoh, are doomed.‚ÄĚ
So Pharaoh wanted to scare the Israelites out of the land ňĻof Egyptňļ, but We drowned him and all of those with him.
And We said to the Children of Israel after Pharaoh, ‚ÄúReside in the land, but when the promise of the Hereafter comes to pass, <sup foot_note=538>538</sup> We will bring you all together.‚ÄĚ

foot_notes.538: This may also refer to the second warning in 17:7.

text: We have sent down the Quran in truth, and with the truth it has come down. We have sent you ňĻO Prophetňļ only as a deliverer of good news and a warner.
ňĻIt isňļ a Quran We have revealed in stages so that you may recite it to people at a deliberate pace. And We have sent it down in successive revelations.

Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúBelieve in this ňĻQuranňļ, or do not. Indeed, when it is recited to those who were gifted with knowledge <sup foot_note=539>539</sup> before it ňĻwas revealedňļ, they fall upon their faces in prostration,

foot_notes.539: i.e., the faithful among the scholars of the Torah and the Gospel.

text: and say, ‚ÄėGlory be to our Lord! Surely the promise of our Lord has been fulfilled.‚Äô
And they fall down upon their faces weeping, and it increases them in humility.‚ÄĚ

Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúCall upon Allah or call upon the Most Compassionate‚ÄĒwhichever you call, He has the Most Beautiful Names.‚ÄĚ Do not recite your prayers too loudly or silently, but seek a way between.

And say, ‚ÄúAll praise is for Allah, Who has never had ňĻanyňļ offspring; <sup foot_note=540>540</sup> nor does He have a partner in ňĻgoverningňļ the kingdom; <sup foot_note=541>541</sup> nor is He pathetic, needing a protector. <sup foot_note=542>542</sup> And revere Him immensely.‚ÄĚ

foot_notes.540: i.e., Jesus, Ezra, or the angels.
foot_notes.541: i.e., another god equal to Him.
